title: Homotopy Rigidity of Nearby Lagrangian Cocores
speaker: Johan Asplund
abstract:
An exact Lagrangian in a cotangent bundle that coincides with
a cotangent fiber outside a compact set, and is disjoint from at least
one cotangent fiber is called a nearby Lagrangian fiber. I will explain
joint work in preparation with Yash Deshmukh and Alex Pieloch, showing
that any nearby Lagrangian fiber in T∗ℂℙ2
 or T∗(Sn−k×ℝk)
 is
smoothly isotopic to a cotangent fiber rel boundary in the complement of
the missed fiber. These results follows from a more general homotopy
rigidity result for exact Lagrangian fillings of unknots in subcritical
Weinstein manifolds.

proof_stages.label: Nearby Lagrangian cocores and the unknotting question
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates the Lagrangian-unknotting problem in a Weinstein handle decomposition, defines shifted cocores and nearby cocores, and motivates the disjointness-from-cocores condition.

proof_stages.label: Specific rigidity results and the skeletal nullhomotopy theorem
proof_stages.summary: The announced smooth-isotopy results for selected cotangent bundles and plumbings are reduced to a general theorem saying that the skeletal projection of a nearby cocore becomes null after passing one further step down the skeleton, under high-dimensional and stable-polarizability hypotheses.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to fillings of a Legendrian unknot in the subcritical part
proof_stages.summary: By flowing away from the critical handles, the nearby cocore and the shifted cocore become two exact Lagrangian fillings of the same Legendrian unknot in the subcritical Weinstein piece, and Floer-theoretic comparison gives that the arbitrary filling is a homotopy disk.

proof_stages.label: Closing the fillings to homotopy spheres and obtaining Fukaya equivalence
proof_stages.summary: A new handle is attached along the common Legendrian boundary so that the two fillings cap off to homotopy spheres, and the new cocore generates the wrapped Fukaya category, implying that the capped spheres are isomorphic after suitable local-system choices.

proof_stages.label: Spectral Fukaya coefficients and open-closed comparison
proof_stages.summary: The ordinary Fukaya equivalence is upgraded to a spectral-coefficient setting using Thom spectra associated to connected covers of the stable orthogonal group, and an open-closed result identifies isomorphic branes by equality of their generalized fundamental classes.

proof_stages.label: Pushforward to the skeleton and stable-homotopy obstruction calculation
proof_stages.summary: Extending the skeletal projection over the added handle and pushing forward the equal fundamental classes yields a zero class whose image in the quotient wedge of spheres is identified, in the stable range, with the homotopy class of the projection map, proving the required nullhomotopy.

proof_stages.label: Applications and h-principle upgrade to smooth isotopy
proof_stages.summary: For the stated cotangent-bundle and plumbing cases, the nullhomotopy obstruction vanishes by the relevant stable-homotopy computations, and an h-principle upgrades the resulting homotopy statement to smooth isotopy rel boundary.

examples.name: Fiber-zero-section surgery obstruction
examples.note: The Lagrangian surgery of a cotangent fiber with the zero section shows that merely agreeing with a fiber at infinity is insufficient, forcing the added hypothesis that the Lagrangian miss some cocore/fiber.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker constructs the Lagrangian surgery of a fiber union the zero section and checks its consequences: it agrees with the fiber at infinity, intersects all fibers, and projects with degree one to the zero section.
